date: Year 1 of Second Emperor (209 BCE) - July
event: Chen Sheng rebelled in former Chu territory, declared himself King of Chu (Zhang Chu)
Wu Chen declared himself King of Zhao; Wei Jiu became King of Wei; Tian Dan became King of Qi
Liu Bang (Pei Gong) rose in Pei; Xiang Liang rose in Kuaiji

date: Year 2 of Second Emperor (208 BCE) - Winter
event: Zhang Han defeated Zhou Zhang's army of 100,000
Zhang Han killed Chen Sheng at Chengfu, defeated Xiang Liang at Dingtao, destroyed Wei Jiu at Linji
Right Chancellor Quji and General Feng Jie committed suicide after failed remonstration

date: Year 3 of Second Emperor (207 BCE) - Winter
Year 3 of Second Emperor (207 BCE) - Summer
event: Zhang Han surrendered to Xiang Yu after defeat at Julu

date: Year 3 of Second Emperor (207 BCE) - 8th month
event: Zhao Gao's 'deer-horse' test; then forced Second Emperor to commit suicide at Wangyi Palace
Ziying killed Zhao Gao, became King of Qin (not Emperor)
Ziying surrendered to Liu Bang at Bashang after 46 days as king
Xiang Yu killed Ziying and Qin royal family, burned Xianyang palaces, divided former Qin territory into Three Qins (Yong, Sai, Zhai)
